results.question: Across the first 3 laps (warmup) and final 3 laps (cliff) of every medium stint at Suzuka 2025 — where 2025 saw 'virtually no drop in lap time on hard and medium' — quantify whether McLaren and Red Bull are exploiting different ends of the stint.
results.author_note: f1technical low-deg analysis + Autosport Strategy Corner piece on 2025 deg patterns.
results.adequacyReason: Answer appears to address the question.
results.answer: Lando NORRIS stint 1: MEDIUM laps 1-21 (21 laps). Oscar PIASTRI stint 1: MEDIUM laps 1-20 (20 laps). Max VERSTAPPEN stint 1: MEDIUM laps 1-21 (21 laps). Yuki TSUNODA stint 1: MEDIUM laps 1-23 (23 laps).
results.answerReasoning: The rows provide warmup and cliff averages, bests, and standard deviations for all four drivers. McLaren's negative cliff_vs_warmup_delta values (−1.564 and −1.444 s) combined with high warmup stddevs show slow, variable early laps and fast, stable late laps — a back-end exploitation pattern. Verstappen's smaller delta (−1.012 s) and low warmup stddev (0.682 s) show he was already quick early, exploiting the front end. Tsunoda's positive cliff_avg_vs_warmup_best (0.261 s) means his cliff average never beat his warmup best, an anomaly likely tied to his high warmup stddev from outlap/traffic effects. | answer_guard:stint_length_focus
results.generationNotes: The query targets session 10006 (Suzuka 2025) and filters core.stint_summary for MEDIUM compound stints belonging to McLaren or Red Bull Racing with at least 6 valid laps (so warmup and cliff windows don't overlap). It then joins core.laps_enriched to pull individual valid, non-pit laps and labels each lap as 'warmup' (first 3 laps of the stint: lap_number <= lap_start + 2) or 'cliff' (last 3 laps: lap_number >= lap_end - 2). Phase-level aggregates (avg, best, stddev) are computed and pivoted so each stint produces one row with both warmup and cliff metrics side-by-side. The final delta column (cliff_avg_s - warmup_avg_s) directly quantifies whether a team is exploiting the opening (low warmup delta) or suffering at the end (high cliff delta), enabling a direct McLaren vs Red Bull comparison of stint exploitation patterns. | session_pin_verified(session_key=10006)
results.sql:
WITH medium_stints AS (
    SELECT
        ss.session_key,
        ss.driver_number,
        ss.driver_name,
        ss.team_name,
        ss.stint_number,
        ss.compound_name,
        ss.lap_start,
        ss.lap_end,
        ss.stint_length_laps
    FROM core.stint_summary ss
    WHERE ss.session_key = 10006
      AND ss.compound_name ILIKE '%MEDIUM%'
      AND ss.team_name IN ('McLaren', 'Red Bull Racing')
      AND ss.stint_length_laps >= 6  -- need at least 6 laps to have distinct warmup and cliff windows
),
lap_windows AS (
    SELECT
        ms.driver_number,
        ms.driver_name,
        ms.team_name,
        ms.stint_number,
        ms.compound_name,
        ms.lap_start,
        ms.lap_end,
        ms.stint_length_laps,
        le.lap_number,
        le.lap_duration,
        le.tyre_age_on_lap,
        CASE
            WHEN le.lap_number <= ms.lap_start + 2 THEN 'warmup'
            WHEN le.lap_number >= ms.lap_end - 2   THEN 'cliff'
            ELSE 'mid'
        END AS stint_phase
    FROM medium_stints ms
    JOIN core.laps_enriched le
        ON le.session_key = ms.session_key
       AND le.driver_number = ms.driver_number
       AND le.lap_number BETWEEN ms.lap_start AND ms.lap_end
       AND le.is_valid = TRUE
       AND le.is_pit_out_lap = FALSE
       AND le.is_pit_lap = FALSE
),
phase_agg AS (
    SELECT
        driver_number,
        driver_name,
        team_name,
        stint_number,
        compound_name,
        lap_start,
        lap_end,
        stint_length_laps,
        stint_phase,
        COUNT(*)                          AS valid_laps,
        ROUND(AVG(lap_duration)::numeric, 3)  AS avg_lap_s,
        ROUND(MIN(lap_duration)::numeric, 3)  AS best_lap_s,
        ROUND(MAX(lap_duration)::numeric, 3)  AS worst_lap_s,
        ROUND(STDDEV(lap_duration)::numeric, 3) AS stddev_lap_s
    FROM lap_windows
    WHERE stint_phase IN ('warmup', 'cliff')
    GROUP BY
        driver_number, driver_name, team_name,
        stint_number, compound_name, lap_start, lap_end, stint_length_laps,
        stint_phase
),
pivoted AS (
    SELECT
        pa.driver_number,
        pa.driver_name,
        pa.team_name,
        pa.stint_number,
        pa.compound_name,
        pa.lap_start,
        pa.lap_end,
        pa.stint_length_laps,
        MAX(CASE WHEN pa.stint_phase = 'warmup' THEN pa.valid_laps  END) AS warmup_valid_laps,
        MAX(CASE WHEN pa.stint_phase = 'warmup' THEN pa.avg_lap_s   END) AS warmup_avg_s,
        MAX(CASE WHEN pa.stint_phase = 'warmup' THEN pa.best_lap_s  END) AS warmup_best_s,
        MAX(CASE WHEN pa.stint_phase = 'warmup' THEN pa.stddev_lap_s END) AS warmup_stddev_s,
        MAX(CASE WHEN pa.stint_phase = 'cliff'  THEN pa.valid_laps  END) AS cliff_valid_laps,
        MAX(CASE WHEN pa.stint_phase = 'cliff'  THEN pa.avg_lap_s   END) AS cliff_avg_s,
        MAX(CASE WHEN pa.stint_phase = 'cliff'  THEN pa.best_lap_s  END) AS cliff_best_s,
        MAX(CASE WHEN pa.stint_phase = 'cliff'  THEN pa.stddev_lap_s END) AS cliff_stddev_s
    FROM phase_agg pa
    GROUP BY
        pa.driver_number, pa.driver_name, pa.team_name,
        pa.stint_number, pa.compound_name, pa.lap_start, pa.lap_end, pa.stint_length_laps
)
SELECT
    p.team_name,
    p.driver_name,
    p.stint_number,
    p.compound_name,
    p.lap_start,
    p.lap_end,
    p.stint_length_laps,
    p.warmup_valid_laps,
    p.warmup_avg_s,
    p.warmup_best_s,
    p.warmup_stddev_s,
    p.cliff_valid_laps,
    p.cliff_avg_s,
    p.cliff_best_s,
    p.cliff_stddev_s,
    ROUND((p.cliff_avg_s - p.warmup_avg_s)::numeric, 3)  AS cliff_vs_warmup_delta_s,
    ROUND((p.cliff_avg_s - p.warmup_best_s)::numeric, 3) AS cliff_avg_vs_warmup_best_s
FROM pivoted p
ORDER BY p.team_name, p.driver_name, p.stint_number
LIMIT 100

results.question: Was the 2025 Hungarian Grand Prix run wet or dry?
results.author_note: F1Technical / Pirelli: rain just before Hungary qualifying; race never wet enough for inters.
results.adequacyReason: Answer appears to address the question.
results.answer: The 2025 Hungarian Grand Prix was run in dry conditions. Across 136 weather samples, no rainfall was detected at any point during the session. The average track temperature was 32.0°C, air temperature 22.1°C, and humidity 57.1%.
results.answerReasoning: The query returns a single row for session_key 9928 showing any_rain_detected = 0 and pct_samples_with_rain = 0.0%, confirming completely dry conditions throughout the race.
results.generationNotes: To determine whether the 2025 Hungarian Grand Prix was wet or dry, I query raw.weather for session_key 9928. The 'rainfall' boolean column indicates whether rain was present. I aggregate across all weather samples to compute the percentage of samples with rain, whether any rain was detected at all, and average temperature/humidity for context. The CASE expression produces a human-readable session condition label. | PostgreSQL's ROUND() function does not accept double precision as its first argument — it requires numeric. The fix is to cast the AVG() results to ::numeric before passing them to ROUND(), which resolves the 'function round(double precision, integer) does not exist' error. | auto_repair_applied | session_pin_verified(session_key=9928)
results.sql:
SELECT
  w.session_key,
  COUNT(*) AS weather_samples,
  ROUND(AVG(w.rainfall::int)::numeric * 100, 1) AS pct_samples_with_rain,
  MAX(w.rainfall::int) AS any_rain_detected,
  ROUND(AVG(w.track_temperature)::numeric, 1) AS avg_track_temp_c,
  ROUND(AVG(w.air_temperature)::numeric, 1) AS avg_air_temp_c,
  ROUND(AVG(w.humidity)::numeric, 1) AS avg_humidity_pct,
  CASE
    WHEN MAX(w.rainfall::int) = 1 THEN 'WET (rain recorded)'
    ELSE 'DRY (no rain recorded)'
  END AS session_condition
FROM raw.weather w
WHERE w.session_key = 9928
GROUP BY w.session_key

results.question: How much pace did Mercedes give up to Red Bull on lap-by-lap delta after the lap-29 inter-to-medium crossover at Spa 2025?
results.author_note: F1.com Belgian GP report: Hamilton one of first to switch from inters; Russell P5 lonely; Verstappen P4 1.5s off podium.
results.adequacyReason: Answer appears to address the question.
results.answer: After the lap-29 inter-to-medium crossover at Spa 2025, the pace delta between Mercedes and Red Bull was mixed rather than consistently in Red Bull's favour. On laps 30–31, Red Bull was faster by ~0.13–0.14s. Laps 32–33 swung to Mercedes (up to 1.06s faster on lap 32, likely a one-off outlier). From laps 34–36, Red Bull reasserted an advantage of 0.07–0.61s. Laps 37–38 were near-equal or marginally Mercedes, then Red Bull pulled ahead again on laps 39–41 by 0.10–0.31s. In the final laps (42–44), Mercedes closed strongly, ending up 0.76–0.85s faster on laps 43–44, possibly reflecting Red Bull tyre degradation. Overall, Red Bull held a modest pace edge of roughly 0.1–0.5s through the middle stint, but Mercedes recovered pace in the closing laps.
results.answerReasoning: The 15 rows cover laps 30–44 post-crossover. Red Bull was faster on 8 of 15 laps, typically by 0.07–0.61s, while Mercedes was faster on 7 laps. The largest Red Bull advantage was lap 34 (+0.609s for RB). The largest Mercedes advantage was lap 32 (-1.060s, likely an anomaly) and laps 43–44 (-0.757s, -0.848s). The data shows no single dominant team but a slight Red Bull edge in the mid-stint phase.
results.generationNotes: The question asks for lap-by-lap pace delta between Mercedes and Red Bull after the lap-29 inter-to-medium crossover at Spa 2025 (session_key=9939). I filter core.laps_enriched for laps > 29, valid laps only (excluding pit-in/pit-out laps), for drivers from both teams. I then compute per-lap average lap times for each team and join them to produce a lap-by-lap delta (positive = Red Bull faster). This gives a clean view of how much pace Mercedes gave up to Red Bull on each lap after the crossover. Note: if only one team's drivers are present in the session data, the FULL OUTER JOIN with the NULL filter will return no rows, indicating data availability limits. | session_pin_verified(session_key=9939)
results.sql:
WITH spa_drivers AS (
  SELECT
    sd.driver_number,
    sd.full_name,
    sd.team_name
  FROM core.session_drivers sd
  WHERE sd.session_key = 9939
    AND sd.team_name IN ('Mercedes', 'Red Bull Racing', 'Oracle Red Bull Racing', 'Mercedes-AMG Petronas F1 Team')
),
post_crossover_laps AS (
  SELECT
    le.driver_number,
    le.driver_name,
    le.team_name,
    le.lap_number,
    le.lap_duration,
    le.compound_name,
    le.is_valid,
    le.is_pit_out_lap,
    le.is_pit_lap,
    le.tyre_age_on_lap
  FROM core.laps_enriched le
  WHERE le.session_key = 9939
    AND le.lap_number > 29
    AND le.is_valid = TRUE
    AND le.is_pit_out_lap = FALSE
    AND le.is_pit_lap = FALSE
    AND le.driver_number IN (SELECT driver_number FROM spa_drivers)
),
mercs AS (
  SELECT
    lap_number,
    AVG(lap_duration) AS merc_avg_lap
  FROM post_crossover_laps
  WHERE team_name ILIKE '%Mercedes%'
  GROUP BY lap_number
),
redbulls AS (
  SELECT
    lap_number,
    AVG(lap_duration) AS rb_avg_lap
  FROM post_crossover_laps
  WHERE team_name ILIKE '%Red Bull%'
  GROUP BY lap_number
)
SELECT
  COALESCE(m.lap_number, r.lap_number) AS lap_number,
  ROUND(m.merc_avg_lap::numeric, 3) AS mercedes_lap_s,
  ROUND(r.rb_avg_lap::numeric, 3) AS redbull_lap_s,
  ROUND((m.merc_avg_lap - r.rb_avg_lap)::numeric, 3) AS merc_delta_to_rb_s,
  CASE
    WHEN (m.merc_avg_lap - r.rb_avg_lap) > 0 THEN 'Red Bull faster'
    WHEN (m.merc_avg_lap - r.rb_avg_lap) < 0 THEN 'Mercedes faster'
    ELSE 'Equal'
  END AS pace_leader
FROM mercs m
FULL OUTER JOIN redbulls r ON m.lap_number = r.lap_number
WHERE m.merc_avg_lap IS NOT NULL AND r.rb_avg_lap IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY lap_number ASC
LIMIT 100

results.question: What was the inter-to-slick crossover lap for the McLarens at Australia 2025, and did Piastri's spin happen on the same lap as his slick-tyre call?
results.author_note: McLaren race report: both McLarens slid; Norris pitted exiting T14, Piastri spun into the grass.
results.adequacyReason: Answer appears to address the question.
results.answer: For both McLarens at Australia 2025, the inter-to-slick crossover lap was lap 34 (same as the slick-to-inter crossover, suggesting a brief or coincident transition window). However, the data does not contain a recorded slick-tyre stint start lap for Piastri (it returned null), and no spin incident for Piastri was found in the race control incidents table. Therefore, it is not possible to confirm whether Piastri's spin occurred on the same lap as his slick-tyre call based on the available data.
results.answerReasoning: Both rows show inter_to_slick_crossover_lap = 34 for Norris and Piastri. Piastri's slick_stint_start_lap is null (no dry-compound stint found in stint_summary) and piastri_spin_lap is null (no matching race control incident), so the spin_on_same_lap_as_slick_call field is null — meaning the question about the spin cannot be answered from these rows.
results.generationNotes: This query has three parts: (1) Pull inter_to_slick_crossover_lap from analytics.weather_impact for all McLaren drivers at session 9693. (2) Find Piastri's (driver 81) first slick-tyre stint start lap from core.stint_summary, filtering out INTER and WET compounds. (3) Look for any spin-related race control incidents for Piastri in analytics.race_control_incidents. The final SELECT joins these together and adds a boolean flag checking whether Piastri's spin incident lap matches his slick-tyre call lap, directly answering both parts of the question. | session_pin_verified(session_key=9693)
results.sql:
WITH mclaren_drivers AS (
  SELECT sd.driver_number, sd.full_name
  FROM core.session_drivers sd
  WHERE sd.session_key = 9693
    AND sd.team_name ILIKE '%McLaren%'
),
inter_to_slick AS (
  SELECT
    wi.driver_number,
    md.full_name,
    wi.inter_to_slick_crossover_lap,
    wi.slick_to_inter_crossover_lap
  FROM analytics.weather_impact wi
  JOIN mclaren_drivers md ON wi.driver_number = md.driver_number
  WHERE wi.session_key = 9693
  GROUP BY wi.driver_number, md.full_name, wi.inter_to_slick_crossover_lap, wi.slick_to_inter_crossover_lap
),
piastri_slick_call AS (
  SELECT
    ss.driver_number,
    ss.driver_name,
    ss.stint_number,
    ss.compound_name,
    ss.lap_start AS slick_stint_start_lap
  FROM core.stint_summary ss
  WHERE ss.session_key = 9693
    AND ss.driver_number = 81
    AND ss.compound_name NOT ILIKE '%INTER%'
    AND ss.compound_name NOT ILIKE '%WET%'
  ORDER BY ss.lap_start ASC
  LIMIT 1
),
piastri_incidents AS (
  SELECT
    rci.driver_number,
    rci.lap_number AS incident_lap,
    rci.incident_kind,
    rci.message_text
  FROM analytics.race_control_incidents rci
  WHERE rci.session_key = 9693
    AND rci.driver_number = 81
    AND (rci.message_text ILIKE '%spin%' OR rci.incident_kind ILIKE '%spin%')
)
SELECT
  its.driver_number,
  its.full_name,
  its.slick_to_inter_crossover_lap,
  its.inter_to_slick_crossover_lap,
  psc.slick_stint_start_lap AS piastri_slick_call_lap,
  pi.incident_lap AS piastri_spin_lap,
  pi.incident_kind,
  pi.message_text,
  CASE
    WHEN its.driver_number = 81
      AND pi.incident_lap IS NOT NULL
      AND psc.slick_stint_start_lap IS NOT NULL
      AND pi.incident_lap = psc.slick_stint_start_lap
    THEN TRUE
    WHEN its.driver_number = 81
      AND (pi.incident_lap IS NULL OR psc.slick_stint_start_lap IS NULL)
    THEN NULL
    ELSE FALSE
  END AS spin_on_same_lap_as_slick_call
FROM inter_to_slick its
LEFT JOIN piastri_slick_call psc ON its.driver_number = psc.driver_number
LEFT JOIN piastri_incidents pi ON its.driver_number = pi.driver_number
ORDER BY its.driver_number

results.question: On the lap-22 dry-line restart at Silverstone 2025, how much time did Verstappen lose to Piastri across that single lap relative to his pre-spin pace average?
results.author_note: RaceFans Verstappen interview: Silverstone restart spin due to set-up, not Piastri incident.
results.adequacyReason: The query returned no rows, so the question remains unanswered.
results.answer: No rows matched this question with the current context.
results.generationNotes: The question asks how much time Verstappen lost to Piastri on lap 22 (the dry-line restart lap at Silverstone 2025, session_key=9947) relative to his own pre-lap-22 average pace. I build three CTEs: (1) lap22 pulls both drivers' (Verstappen=1, Piastri=81) lap 22 durations; (2) pre_spin_pace computes Verstappen's average valid, non-pit lap time for laps 1–21 as his baseline; (3) the final SELECT surfaces Verstappen's pre-spin average, both drivers' lap-22 times, the head-to-head gap on lap 22, and Verstappen's lap-22 delta vs his own pre-spin baseline. This gives a complete picture of the time loss on that single restart lap. | session_pin_verified(session_key=9947)
results.sql:
WITH lap22 AS (
    SELECT
        driver_number,
        driver_name,
        team_name,
        lap_number,
        lap_duration,
        is_valid
    FROM core.laps_enriched
    WHERE session_key = 9947
      AND lap_number = 22
      AND driver_number IN (1, 81)
),
pre_spin_pace AS (
    -- Verstappen's average valid lap time on laps before lap 22
    SELECT
        driver_number,
        driver_name,
        AVG(lap_duration) AS avg_pre_lap22_pace
    FROM core.laps_enriched
    WHERE session_key = 9947
      AND driver_number = 1
      AND lap_number < 22
      AND is_valid = TRUE
      AND is_pit_out_lap = FALSE
      AND is_pit_lap = FALSE
    GROUP BY driver_number, driver_name
),
piastri_lap22 AS (
    SELECT lap_duration AS piastri_lap22_time
    FROM lap22
    WHERE driver_number = 81
),
verstappen_lap22 AS (
    SELECT lap_duration AS ver_lap22_time
    FROM lap22
    WHERE driver_number = 1
)
SELECT
    p.driver_number                                      AS verstappen_driver_number,
    p.driver_name                                        AS verstappen_name,
    ROUND(p.avg_pre_lap22_pace::numeric, 3)              AS verstappen_avg_pre_spin_pace_s,
    ROUND(v.ver_lap22_time::numeric, 3)                  AS verstappen_lap22_time_s,
    ROUND(pi.piastri_lap22_time::numeric, 3)             AS piastri_lap22_time_s,
    ROUND((v.ver_lap22_time - pi.piastri_lap22_time)::numeric, 3)
                                                         AS ver_vs_piastri_gap_on_lap22_s,
    ROUND((v.ver_lap22_time - p.avg_pre_lap22_pace)::numeric, 3)
                                                         AS ver_lap22_vs_pre_spin_avg_delta_s,
    ROUND((pi.piastri_lap22_time - p.avg_pre_lap22_pace)::numeric, 3)
                                                         AS piastri_lap22_vs_ver_pre_spin_avg_delta_s
FROM pre_spin_pace p
CROSS JOIN piastri_lap22 pi
CROSS JOIN verstappen_lap22 v

results.question: At the 2025 Belgian GP, compare each top-team's first-stint inter-pace delta to leader and pinpoint who waited too long to switch to slicks — was Hamilton's early switch optimal vs Verstappen's later one?
results.author_note: F1.com Belgian GP report: Piastri won wet-dry Spa, Hamilton early to slicks finished 6th, Verstappen 4th.
results.adequacyReason: The query returned no rows, so the question remains unanswered.
results.answer: No rows matched this question with the current context.
results.generationNotes:
The query builds a multi-stage analysis for session 9939 (2025 Belgian GP) focusing on drivers 1 (Verstappen) and 44 (Hamilton):

1. **session_laps / stint_info**: Pull lap-level and stint-level data for both drivers from core.laps_enriched and core.stint_summary.

2. **leader_laps**: Compute the leader's lap time per lap number across the full field to establish a reference delta.

3. **driver_lap_detail**: Enrich each driver's lap with compound category (INTER/WET/SLICK/OTHER) and delta-to-leader.

4. **switch_laps**: Identify the exact lap each driver pitted from inters to slicks (pit-out lap on a slick compound after having been on inters).

5. **inter_stint_summary / slick_stint_summary**: Summarise the inter stint (avg/best lap, duration) and the first slick stint after inters.

6. **inter_delta_summary**: Compute average and best delta-to-leader while on inters — the core metric for 'who was losing time on inters'.

7. **Final SELECT**: Joins all CTEs to produce a side-by-side comparison: inter stint window, pace delta to leader on inters, switch lap, position at switch, slick pace, and a heuristic assessment of whether the switch timing was optimal. Ordered by switch lap to show who switched first (Hamilton) vs later (Verstappen). | The only fix needed was removing the reference to `le.normalized_compound` in the `session_laps` CTE, which does not exist in `core.laps_enriched`. That column was selected but never actually used downstream in the query — the tyre categorisation logic already uses `compound_name` with ILIKE patterns. Removing the `normalized_compound` line from the SELECT in `session_laps` resolves the validation error without any other logic changes. | auto_repair_applied | session_pin_verified(session_key=9939)
results.sql:
WITH session_laps AS (
  SELECT
    le.driver_number,
    le.driver_name,
    le.team_name,
    le.lap_number,
    le.lap_duration,
    le.compound_name,
    le.stint_number,
    le.tyre_age_on_lap,
    le.is_pit_out_lap,
    le.is_pit_lap,
    le.is_valid,
    le.position_end_of_lap,
    le.track_flag
  FROM core.laps_enriched le
  WHERE le.session_key = 9939
    AND le.driver_number IN (1, 44)
),
stint_info AS (
  SELECT
    ss.driver_number,
    ss.driver_name,
    ss.team_name,
    ss.stint_number,
    ss.compound_name,
    ss.lap_start,
    ss.lap_end,
    ss.avg_valid_lap,
    ss.best_valid_lap,
    ss.stint_length_laps,
    ss.valid_lap_count
  FROM core.stint_summary ss
  WHERE ss.session_key = 9939
    AND ss.driver_number IN (1, 44)
),
leader_laps AS (
  SELECT
    le.lap_number,
    MIN(le.lap_duration) FILTER (WHERE le.is_valid AND le.position_end_of_lap = 1) AS leader_lap_time,
    MIN(le.lap_duration) FILTER (WHERE le.is_valid) AS fastest_lap_on_number
  FROM core.laps_enriched le
  WHERE le.session_key = 9939
    AND le.is_valid
  GROUP BY le.lap_number
),
driver_lap_detail AS (
  SELECT
    sl.driver_number,
    sl.driver_name,
    sl.team_name,
    sl.lap_number,
    sl.lap_duration,
    sl.compound_name,
    sl.stint_number,
    sl.tyre_age_on_lap,
    sl.is_pit_out_lap,
    sl.is_pit_lap,
    sl.is_valid,
    sl.position_end_of_lap,
    sl.track_flag,
    ll.leader_lap_time,
    ll.fastest_lap_on_number,
    CASE
      WHEN sl.is_valid AND ll.leader_lap_time IS NOT NULL
      THEN ROUND((sl.lap_duration - ll.leader_lap_time)::numeric, 3)
      ELSE NULL
    END AS delta_to_leader,
    CASE
      WHEN sl.compound_name ILIKE '%INTER%' THEN 'INTER'
      WHEN sl.compound_name ILIKE '%WET%' THEN 'WET'
      WHEN sl.compound_name ILIKE '%SOFT%' OR sl.compound_name ILIKE '%MEDIUM%' OR sl.compound_name ILIKE '%HARD%' THEN 'SLICK'
      ELSE 'OTHER'
    END AS tyre_category
  FROM session_laps sl
  LEFT JOIN leader_laps ll ON sl.lap_number = ll.lap_number
),
switch_laps AS (
  SELECT
    d1.driver_number,
    d1.driver_name,
    d1.lap_number AS switch_lap,
    d1.compound_name AS new_compound,
    d1.position_end_of_lap AS position_at_switch
  FROM driver_lap_detail d1
  WHERE d1.tyre_category = 'SLICK'
    AND d1.is_pit_out_lap = TRUE
    AND EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM driver_lap_detail d2
      WHERE d2.driver_number = d1.driver_number
        AND d2.lap_number < d1.lap_number
        AND d2.tyre_category = 'INTER'
    )
),
inter_stint_summary AS (
  SELECT
    si.driver_number,
    si.driver_name,
    si.team_name,
    si.stint_number,
    si.compound_name,
    si.lap_start AS inter_stint_start_lap,
    si.lap_end AS inter_stint_end_lap,
    si.stint_length_laps AS inter_stint_laps,
    si.avg_valid_lap AS inter_avg_lap,
    si.best_valid_lap AS inter_best_lap
  FROM stint_info si
  WHERE si.compound_name ILIKE '%INTER%'
),
slick_stint_summary AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT ON (si.driver_number)
    si.driver_number,
    si.stint_number,
    si.compound_name AS slick_compound,
    si.lap_start AS slick_stint_start_lap,
    si.avg_valid_lap AS slick_avg_lap,
    si.best_valid_lap AS slick_best_lap
  FROM stint_info si
  WHERE (si.compound_name ILIKE '%SOFT%' OR si.compound_name ILIKE '%MEDIUM%' OR si.compound_name ILIKE '%HARD%')
    AND EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM inter_stint_summary iss
      WHERE iss.driver_number = si.driver_number
        AND si.lap_start > iss.inter_stint_start_lap
    )
  ORDER BY si.driver_number, si.lap_start ASC
),
inter_delta_summary AS (
  SELECT
    dl.driver_number,
    dl.driver_name,
    ROUND(AVG(dl.delta_to_leader)::numeric, 3) AS avg_inter_delta_to_leader,
    ROUND(MIN(dl.delta_to_leader)::numeric, 3) AS best_inter_delta_to_leader,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE dl.is_valid) AS inter_valid_laps
  FROM driver_lap_detail dl
  WHERE dl.tyre_category = 'INTER'
    AND dl.is_valid
  GROUP BY dl.driver_number, dl.driver_name
)
SELECT
  ids.driver_number,
  ids.driver_name,
  iss.team_name,
  iss.inter_stint_start_lap,
  iss.inter_stint_end_lap,
  iss.inter_stint_laps,
  ROUND(iss.inter_avg_lap::numeric, 3) AS inter_avg_lap_s,
  ROUND(iss.inter_best_lap::numeric, 3) AS inter_best_lap_s,
  ids.avg_inter_delta_to_leader AS avg_delta_to_leader_on_inters_s,
  ids.best_inter_delta_to_leader AS best_delta_to_leader_on_inters_s,
  ids.inter_valid_laps,
  sl.switch_lap AS slick_switch_lap,
  sl.new_compound AS slick_compound_switched_to,
  sl.position_at_switch,
  sls.slick_avg_lap AS slick_avg_lap_s,
  sls.slick_best_lap AS slick_best_lap_s,
  CASE
    WHEN sl.switch_lap IS NOT NULL AND iss.inter_stint_start_lap IS NOT NULL
    THEN sl.switch_lap - iss.inter_stint_start_lap
    ELSE NULL
  END AS laps_on_inters_before_switch,
  CASE
    WHEN ids.avg_inter_delta_to_leader > 5 THEN 'Likely waited too long — large avg gap on inters'
    WHEN ids.avg_inter_delta_to_leader BETWEEN 2 AND 5 THEN 'Moderate gap on inters — timing debatable'
    WHEN ids.avg_inter_delta_to_leader < 2 THEN 'Competitive on inters — switch timing may have been optimal'
    ELSE 'Insufficient data'
  END AS switch_timing_assessment
FROM inter_delta_summary ids
JOIN inter_stint_summary iss ON ids.driver_number = iss.driver_number
LEFT JOIN switch_laps sl ON ids.driver_number = sl.driver_number
LEFT JOIN slick_stint_summary sls ON ids.driver_number = sls.driver_number
ORDER BY sl.switch_lap ASC NULLS LAST
LIMIT 20

results.question: For the 2025 British GP, identify the inters-to-slicks (and slicks-to-inters) crossover-lap windows during each rain band and quantify the position swing for any driver who pitted off-window.
results.author_note: F1.com / Mercedes / McLaren British GP reports: 5 drivers gambled on slicks at start; Hülkenberg's maiden podium.
results.adequacyReason: Answer appears to address the question.
results.answer: For the 2025 British GP (session 9947), the data identifies two main crossover windows: an inter-to-slick window around lap 3–7 and a slick-to-inter window around lap 10–11. All recorded wet-compound transitions were assessed as 'on_window' (within ±2 laps of the crossover). Kimi Antonelli (Mercedes) pitted from inters to slicks on lap 2 (1 lap early, crossover lap 3) and dropped 10 positions by lap 5 (P4 → P14), suggesting the track was not yet ready for slicks despite being technically near-window. Lance Stroll (Aston Martin) pitted inter-to-slick on lap 6 (1 lap early vs. crossover lap 7) and lost 3 positions (P8 → P11), then switched back to inters on lap 10 and gained 1 position (P5 → P4). The slick-to-inter crossover around lap 10–11 saw multiple drivers (Antonelli, Bearman, Stroll, Leclerc, Russell) all pit on lap 10, all classified on-window; no driver pitted demonstrably off-window in this dataset, so no large off-window position swings are recorded beyond Antonelli's early inter-to-slick call.
results.answerReasoning: The rows show all 14 entries have timing_assessment = 'on_window' (laps_off_window = -1, i.e., 1 lap early). The largest position swing is Antonelli's +10 (positions lost) after his lap-2 inter-to-slick pit, which was 1 lap before the crossover lap of 3. Stroll lost 3 positions on his lap-6 inter-to-slick (crossover lap 7). The slick-to-inter crossover window is around lap 10–11 based on slick_to_inter_crossover_lap values of 10–11 across drivers. No driver pitted more than 2 laps off-window, so no 'pitted_late' or 'pitted_early' classifications exist in the data.
results.generationNotes:
The query addresses the 2025 British GP (session_key=9947) crossover-lap analysis in four steps:

1. **Crossover windows** — Pull `slick_to_inter_crossover_lap`, `inter_to_slick_crossover_lap`, and `crossover_lap` from `analytics.weather_impact` (aggregated per driver since these values repeat on every row). Also capture wet-lap count and average wet pace delta to characterise each rain band.

2. **Pit transitions** — From `core.laps_enriched`, identify laps where `is_pit_lap = TRUE` and classify each as `slick_to_inter` or `inter_to_slick` based on the compound before and after the stop (using LEAD to peek at the next stint's compound).

3. **Off-window assessment** — Join pit transitions to crossover windows and compute `laps_off_window` (positive = pitted late, negative = pitted early). A driver is considered 'on_window' if within ±2 laps of the relevant crossover lap.

4. **Position swing** — Compare `position_end_of_lap` at the pit lap vs 3 laps later (allowing time for the new tyre to show its effect) to quantify the position gain/loss associated with the timing decision.

The query covers all drivers in the session (not just driver 1) since the question asks about the field-wide crossover windows and off-window pitters. | session_pin_verified(session_key=9947)
results.sql:
WITH session_drivers AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT driver_number, driver_name, team_name
  FROM analytics.weather_impact
  WHERE session_key = 9947
),

-- Get crossover laps per driver from weather_impact
crossover_windows AS (
  SELECT
    driver_number,
    driver_name,
    team_name,
    MIN(slick_to_inter_crossover_lap)   AS slick_to_inter_crossover_lap,
    MIN(inter_to_slick_crossover_lap)   AS inter_to_slick_crossover_lap,
    MIN(crossover_lap)                  AS crossover_lap,
    COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_wet_lap)  AS wet_lap_count,
    AVG(wet_pace_delta_s) FILTER (WHERE is_wet_lap AND wet_pace_delta_s IS NOT NULL) AS avg_wet_pace_delta_s
  FROM analytics.weather_impact
  WHERE session_key = 9947
  GROUP BY driver_number, driver_name, team_name
),

-- Identify actual pit stops and the compound transitions
pit_transitions AS (
  SELECT
    le.driver_number,
    le.driver_name,
    le.team_name,
    le.lap_number                        AS pit_lap,
    le.compound_name                     AS compound_before,
    LEAD(le.compound_name) OVER (
      PARTITION BY le.driver_number ORDER BY le.lap_number
    )                                    AS compound_after,
    le.position_end_of_lap               AS position_at_pit
  FROM core.laps_enriched le
  WHERE le.session_key = 9947
    AND le.is_pit_lap = TRUE
),

-- Classify each pit as slick->inter, inter->slick, or other
pit_classified AS (
  SELECT
    pt.*,
    CASE
      WHEN pt.compound_before ILIKE '%INTER%' AND pt.compound_after NOT ILIKE '%INTER%'
        THEN 'inter_to_slick'
      WHEN pt.compound_before NOT ILIKE '%INTER%' AND pt.compound_after ILIKE '%INTER%'
        THEN 'slick_to_inter'
      ELSE 'other'
    END AS transition_type
  FROM pit_transitions pt
  WHERE pt.compound_after IS NOT NULL
    AND (
      (pt.compound_before ILIKE '%INTER%' AND pt.compound_after NOT ILIKE '%INTER%')
      OR
      (pt.compound_before NOT ILIKE '%INTER%' AND pt.compound_after ILIKE '%INTER%')
    )
),

-- Join pit transitions with crossover windows to assess timing
pit_vs_window AS (
  SELECT
    pc.driver_number,
    pc.driver_name,
    pc.team_name,
    pc.pit_lap,
    pc.compound_before,
    pc.compound_after,
    pc.transition_type,
    pc.position_at_pit,
    cw.slick_to_inter_crossover_lap,
    cw.inter_to_slick_crossover_lap,
    cw.crossover_lap,
    cw.avg_wet_pace_delta_s,
    CASE
      WHEN pc.transition_type = 'slick_to_inter'
        THEN pc.pit_lap - cw.slick_to_inter_crossover_lap
      WHEN pc.transition_type = 'inter_to_slick'
        THEN pc.pit_lap - cw.inter_to_slick_crossover_lap
      ELSE NULL
    END AS laps_off_window
  FROM pit_classified pc
  LEFT JOIN crossover_windows cw USING (driver_number)
),

-- Get position swing: position before pit vs position 3 laps after pit
position_swing AS (
  SELECT
    pvw.*,
    rps_after.position_end_of_lap AS position_3_laps_after,
    rps_after.position_end_of_lap - pvw.position_at_pit AS position_swing
  FROM pit_vs_window pvw
  LEFT JOIN core.laps_enriched rps_after
    ON rps_after.session_key = 9947
    AND rps_after.driver_number = pvw.driver_number
    AND rps_after.lap_number = pvw.pit_lap + 3
)

SELECT
  ps.driver_number,
  ps.driver_name,
  ps.team_name,
  ps.transition_type,
  ps.pit_lap,
  ps.compound_before,
  ps.compound_after,
  ps.slick_to_inter_crossover_lap,
  ps.inter_to_slick_crossover_lap,
  ps.crossover_lap                                    AS combined_crossover_lap,
  ps.laps_off_window,
  CASE
    WHEN ps.laps_off_window IS NULL THEN 'unknown'
    WHEN ABS(ps.laps_off_window) <= 2 THEN 'on_window'
    WHEN ps.laps_off_window > 2      THEN 'pitted_late'
    ELSE                                  'pitted_early'
  END                                                 AS timing_assessment,
  ps.position_at_pit,
  ps.position_3_laps_after,
  ps.position_swing,
  ROUND(ps.avg_wet_pace_delta_s::numeric, 3)          AS avg_wet_pace_delta_s
FROM position_swing ps
ORDER BY ps.transition_type, ps.pit_lap
LIMIT 200
